graded_findings.ref: When a cluster uses the built-in Metastore backend, cluster metadata is served by a single head node (`metastore_head_node` in `cluster_config.json`). If that node goes down, metadata service is interrupted
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence says this issue element is explicitly addressed by the cited spec text, so it does not indicate a merge-blocking adherence gap. It remains useful context for coverage review.

graded_findings.ref: there is no tooling to reconfigure a cluster for a highly-available, multi-head Metastore
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence says this issue element is explicitly addressed by the cited spec text, so it does not indicate a merge-blocking adherence gap. It remains useful context for coverage review.

graded_findings.ref: Add a `dscli` subcommand that takes an existing `cluster_config.json` and produces a variant configured for a highly-available Metastore — electing several worker nodes as Metastore head replicas so metadata keeps being served if one head fails.
graded_findings.rationale: The gather evidence says this issue element is explicitly addressed by the cited spec text, so it does not indicate a merge-blocking adherence gap. It remains useful context for coverage review.

examined: When a cluster uses the built-in Metastore backend, cluster metadata is served by a single head node (`metastore_head_node` in `cluster_config.json`). If that node goes down, metadata service is interrupted
there is no tooling to reconfigure a cluster for a highly-available, multi-head Metastore

Add a `dscli` subcommand that takes an existing `cluster_config.json` and produces a variant configured for a highly-available Metastore — electing several worker nodes as Metastore head replicas so metadata keeps being served if one head fails.
